zh.lifeSummary: 當David II de Strathbogie 10th Earl of Atholl於1285年出生在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾亨特利時，他的父親Sir John De Strathbogie 9th Earl of Atholl19歲，母親Margaret Mar15歲。他於1308年與Jeanne Comyn在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他於1312年被列為constable of scotland。他去世於1326年12月28日在英國英格蘭肯特爾哈姆 卡素享年41歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When David II de Strathbogie 10th Earl of Atholl was born in 1285, in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John De Strathbogie 9th Earl of Atholl, was 19 and his mother, Margaret Mar, was 15. He married Jeanne Comyn in 1308, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. In 1312, at the age of 27, his occupation is listed as constable of scotland. He died on 28 December 1326, in Chilham Castle, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque David II de Strathbogie 10th Earl of Atholl est né en 1285, à Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John De Strathbogie 9th Earl of Atholl, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Margaret Mar, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Jeanne Comyn en 1308, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. En 1312, à l’âge de 27 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est constable of scotland. Il est décédé le 28 décembre 1326, à Chilham Castle, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.Chilham Castle, Badlesmere, Kent, Englan: Chilham Castle, Kent, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als David II de Strathbogie 10th Earl of Atholl 1285 in Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John De Strathbogie 9th Earl of Atholl 19 und seine Mutter Margaret Mar 15. Er hat 1308 in Scotland Jeanne Comyn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. 1312, als er 27 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als constable of scotland aufgeführt. Er starb am 28. Dezember 1326 im Alter von 41 Jahren in Chilham Castle, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando David II de Strathbogie 10th Earl of Atholl è nato il nel 1285 nel Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir John De Strathbogie 9th Earl of Atholl, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Mar, aveva 15 anni. Lui sposato Jeanne Comyn nel 1308, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Nel 1312, all'età di 27 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come constable of scotland. Morì il 28 dicembre 1326, nel Chilham Castle, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: David II de Strathbogie 10th Earl of Athollが1285年にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアハントリーで生まれた時，父Sir John De Strathbogie 9th Earl of Athollは19歳，母Margaret Marは15歳であった。1308年にスコットランドでJeanne Comynと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。1312年，27歳の時，職業はconstable of scotlandと記録されている。彼は1326年12月28日にイギリスイギリスケントチルハム カストルで41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: David II de Strathbogie 10th Earl of Atholl(이)가 1285년 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 헌틀리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John De Strathbogie 9th Earl of Atholl(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Mar(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1308년 스컷랜드에서 Jeanne Comyn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 1312년, 27세의 나이로 그의 직업은 constable of scotland이었다. 그는 1326년 12월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 치함 카스틀에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando David II de Strathbogie 10th Earl of Atholl nasceu em 1285, em Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John De Strathbogie 9th Earl of Atholl, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Mar, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Jeanne Comyn em 1308, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Em 1312, com 27 anos, sua profissão aparece como constable of scotland. Ele faleceu em 28 de dezembro de 1326, em Chilham Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда David II de Strathbogie 10th Earl of Atholl родился в 1285 г. в местности хантли, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John De Strathbogie 9th Earl of Atholl было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Mar, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Jeanne Comyn в 1308 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. В 1312 году, в возрасте 27 лет,  он работал по профессии constable of scotland. Он умер 28 декабря 1326 г. в местности чилхам кастл, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando David II de Strathbogie 10th Earl of Atholl nació en 1285, en Huntly, Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John De Strathbogie 9th Earl of Atholl, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Margaret Mar, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Jeanne Comyn en 1308, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. En 1312, a la edad de 27 años, su ocupación registrada era constable of scotland. Murió el 28 de diciembre de 1326, en Chilham Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 41 años.
